title: coleus porcatus
intro: Coleus porcatus, also known as Coleus forskohlii, is a tropical plant native to India and Africa, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the heart and increase its contractions, which has led to its use in treating conditions such as glaucoma and high blood pressure. The plant contains a compound called forskolin, which has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in weight loss. Forskolin can be prepared as an herbal supplement or used in topical preparations, such as creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
